items.title: Taiwan expects power demand to increase by more than 5GW by 2030, enough to power nearly 4 million homes — rise in electricity consumption driven by semiconductor manufacturing and AI data center deployments
items.description: New semiconductor fabs and data centers are fueling Taiwan's growing power demand, with the island expected to require more than 5GW of new electricity sources and power infrastructure to keep up.

Subscribe to our newsletter Taiwan is facing a new challenge in its goal to retain its advantage in AI and advanced semiconductor manufacturing — crucial for maintaining its silicon shield — as it grapples with a tightening power supply. Electricity demand is rising sharply as Taiwanese tech companies build semiconductor manufacturing facilities alongside AI data centers. According to Digitimes, both the AI and semiconductor industries could add about 5 gigawatts of demand — enough to power upwards of 3.75 million homes — by 2030, roughly a gigawatt of additional load per year through the end of the decade.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThis expansion, fueled by increasing global demand for the most advanced AI chips, is putting even more pressure on the island’s grid. Taipower chairperson Wen-sheng Tseng notes that each wafer fab needs around 200MW to operate, suggesting that the multiple facilities planned for and being built around the island will demand 5.3 to 5.4 gigawatts of electricity. This does not count the other plants and factories needed to support the operation of these factories, so it’s likely that the power demand will even be higher than projected. Nevertheless, four new electricity-generating gas units are set to come online this year, with an additional 5.2GW expected to enter trials soon. The company is also working to beef up Taiwan’s grid and enhance resilience plans, ensuring that the island’s power supply can keep up with the demand, particularly from science parks and industrial zones.Despite that, Taipower is still warning that demand is also increasing outside of these areas. Some fabs and servers are setting up facilities beyond these designated sectors, sometimes even requesting up to 20% of the power consumption of a science park for just a single structure. Because of this, Tseng says that both fabs and tech companies should be careful when selecting sites for their future infrastructure, ensuring that they’ll only be built in places where there is ample power supply to avoid bottlenecks in the future. You may like U.S. electricity grid stretches thin as data centers rush to turn on onsite generators Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption Even though Taiwan’s power utility company is warning about power consumption, it seems to be on track to cover the future growth of the AI and semiconductor industry, provided that the current projections hold. This is a far cry from what the U.S. is experiencing, with the White House resorting to forcing big tech companies to promise that they will pay for the grid and power source upgrades needed to supply the massive amounts of electricity that AI data centers need. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 台湾电力需求预估2030年前增逾5000兆瓦，相当于近400万户家庭用电——半导体制造和AI数据中心部署成主要驱动因素
items.descriptionZh:
台湾在维持其人工智能（AI）与先进半导体制造优势、巩固其“硅盾”战略地位的过程中，正面临新的挑战：日益紧张的电力供应。随着台湾科技公司同时扩建半导体制造设施与AI数据中心，电力需求正急剧攀升。据《电子时报》报道，到2030年，AI和半导体产业预计将新增约5吉瓦（GW）的电力需求——相当于超过375万户家庭的用电量，这意味着直至本年代末，每年将增加约1吉瓦的负荷。

这一扩张主要由全球对最先进AI芯片的强劲需求驱动，正给台湾电网带来更大压力。台电董事长曾文生指出，每座晶圆厂约需200兆瓦（MW）电力才能运营，这意味着全岛规划中和建设中的多座工厂将需要5.3至5.4吉瓦的电力。这还未计入支持这些工厂运营所需的其他配套厂区，因此实际电力需求很可能高于预期。

为应对这一挑战，台湾今年将有四台新的燃气发电机组投入使用，另有5.2吉瓦发电容量预计很快投入试运行。台电同时正努力加强电网建设并提升韧性计划，以确保电力供应能跟上需求增长，尤其满足科学园区和工业区的用电。

尽管如此，台电仍警告称，这些区域之外的电力需求也在增加。部分晶圆厂和服务器设施正选址在规划区域之外，有时甚至单栋建筑就要求占用科学园区高达20%的用电量。因此，曾文生建议晶圆厂和科技公司未来选址需谨慎，应确保只在电力供应充足的地区建设新设施，以避免未来出现瓶颈。

尽管台电对电力消耗发出警示，但若当前预测准确，台湾似乎仍有望满足AI和半导体产业的未来增长需求。这与美国当前境况形成鲜明对比：白宫已不得不强制要求大型科技公司承诺，为满足AI数据中心庞大电力需求所需的电网和电源升级支付费用。

总体来看，台湾在平衡 AI 与半导体产业发展与能源保障方面虽面临压力，但通过积极扩展发电能力与强化电网，正试图为产业持续增长提供支撑，这与部分国家和地区依赖企业承担基础设施升级成本的模式有所不同。

items.title: Trump summons tech giants to White House to pledge power payment commitments — ‘ratepayer protection plan’ will make data center operators negotiate discrete payment structure for electricity use
items.description: Some of the U.S.'s biggest AI tech companies are expected to go to the White House to sign the "ratepayer protection pledge" that promises they will pay for all of their electricity usage and not pass on the burden of increased rates to the average American.

Subscribe to our newsletter The biggest AI tech companies, including Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and Google, are expected to join President Donald Trump at the White House today to sign the new “ratepayer protection pledge” in a bid to keep electricity prices under control. Trump said during the State of the Union address, “We’re telling the major tech companies that they have the obligation to provide for their own power need.” According to Bloomberg, Meta, Amazon, and OpenAI have confirmed attendance at the White House event on Wednesday, while Google declined to comment.The massive power demands of the AI data centers have upended the electricity market, causing electricity costs for the average American to increase sharply. Some states have reported an increase of up to 36%, with wholesale power prices increasing by 267% in just five years. This is putting undue stress on the ordinary consumer, so much so that both sides of the aisle are demanding action from tech giants.Microsoft was the first to respond to Trump’s call for AI data centers to “pay their own way” when it comes to their electricity consumption, promising to be a “good neighbor” in the communities where it is present. OpenAI soon followed suit, saying that it will fund grid upgrades and apply flexible loads to reduce stress on the grid, while Anthropic said that it will pay 100% of its grid infrastructure costs and even produce new power sources. Now, it seems that the administration wants to formalize these promises. You may like Trump orders Big Tech to build its own power plants to generate power for AI data centers Trump says that AI tech companies need to ‘pay their own way’ when it comes to their electricity consumption Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers Energy Secretary Chris Wright reportedly said in a statement that the pledge "will deliver more affordable, reliable, and secure energy for the American people and help stop the rising electricity prices that started during the previous administration," while ensuring the U.S. wins the AI race.However, experts are skeptical whether these promises and pledges will hold water in the long run. “The ratepayer protection plan is a show designed to sweep this issue under the rug and show the White House has solved the problem,” Director Ari Peskoe of the Harvard Law School Electricity Law Initiative told the publication. “The White House has no real authority here aside from the bully pulpit.” It’s not until we see big tech companies sign separate rate structures with electricity providers, grid operators, and state governments that we will know whether these pledges are effective. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 特朗普召见科技巨头承诺付费方案：白宫拟推“纳税人保护计划”，要求数据中心运营商协商独立用电收费方案。
items.descriptionZh:
美国主要科技巨头，包括亚马逊、Meta、微软和谷歌，预计将于今日齐聚白宫，与总统特朗普共同签署一项名为“费率支付者保护承诺”的新协议，旨在控制因人工智能数据中心激增而导致的电价上涨。特朗普在国情咨文中强调，“我们正告知主要科技公司，他们有责任为自身的电力需求负责。”据彭博社报道，Meta、亚马逊和OpenAI已确认出席周三的白宫活动，而谷歌则拒绝置评。

人工智能数据中心的巨大电力需求已严重扰乱电力市场，导致美国普通居民的电费大幅上涨。部分州报告电价涨幅高达36%，批发电价在短短五年内飙升了267%。这给普通消费者带来了过度压力，以至于两党均要求科技巨头采取行动。

微软率先响应特朗普关于AI数据中心应“自负盈亏”的呼吁，承诺在其运营的社区成为“好邻居”。OpenAI紧随其后，表示将资助电网升级并应用柔性负载以减轻电网压力。Anthropic则承诺承担其100%的电网基础设施成本，甚至开发新的电力来源。目前，美国政府似乎希望将这些承诺正式化。

能源部长克里斯·赖特在一份声明中表示，该承诺“将为美国人民提供更可负担、可靠和安全的能源，帮助遏制始于上届政府的电价上涨趋势”，同时确保美国在人工智能竞赛中保持领先。

然而，专家对这些承诺的长期有效性持怀疑态度。哈佛法学院电力法律倡议主任阿里·佩斯科告诉媒体，“费率支付者保护计划是一场作秀，旨在掩盖问题并显示白宫已解决问题。白宫在此事上除舆论影响力外并无实际权力。”只有当大型科技公司与电力供应商、电网运营商和州政府签署独立的费率结构协议时，我们才能判断这些承诺是否真正有效。

items.title: Nvidia invests $4 billion into photonics firms in a bid to bolster data center interconnect supply chains — Lumentum and Coherent investment to fund U.S. R&D and manufacturing facilities, supports capacity rights and future access
items.description: Nvidia has invested a combined $4 billion USD in photonics and networking companies Lumentum and Coherent in a deal that will see both building U.S. facilities, while locking down access rights and capacity for future technologies.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia has pledged to invest a combined $4 billion in two photonic manufacturing firms, Lumentum and Coherent. As Reuters reports, this continues Nvidia's plans to expend capital investment with its expansive cash reserves to help build the AI ecosystem and improve the output of its models. The quiet part is that it also helps keep Nvidia on the cutting edge.Both companies develop optical and semiconductor technologies, enabling future networking hardware and AI chip designs that utilize light to transfer data, rather than electricity. With Nvidia looking to continue providing the digital shovels in this AI gold rush, leveraging forward-looking technologies like photonics may be one way it maintains its edge in the range of hardware spaces it now occupies in the global AI buildout.“AI has reinvented computing and is driving the largest computing infrastructure buildout in history,” said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang. “Together with Lumentum, NVIDIA is advancing the world’s most sophisticated silicon photonics to build the next generation of gigawatt-scale AI factories.” You may like Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneck Marvell’s $5.5B Celestial AI acquisition expands its role in AI data center hardware Nvidia pumps another $2 billion into CoreWeave and announces standalone availability of Vera CPU Jim Anderson, CEO of Coherent, spoke on their deal, stating: “This strategic relationship underscores Coherent’s role as a key enabler of next-generation AI data center infrastructure. We are proud to expand our 20-year relationship with NVIDIA by increasing their access to include multiple product families to help them build the AI data centers of the future.”Cash, access, capacityNvidia has been working with both of these companies for some time on its own networking hardware and to develop a strong supply chain for these key components. These new deals only enhance that cooperation.As with most of the deals within and without the AI industry over the last year, Nvidia's investment in these firms is a mixture of hard currency, future purchase orders, and secured access to key technologies. With Lumentum, Nvidia's $2 billion investment will support its R&D and manufacturing expansion in the U.S. with a new fabrication facility. It also includes a "multibillion purchase commitment" from Nvidia, and "future capacity access rights for advanced laser components."The Coherent deal reads almost identically. There, Nvidia and Coherant talk up the "multiyear strategic agreement" between the two companies, which includes "an Nvidia multibillion-dollar purchase commitment and future access and capacity rights for advanced laser and optical networking products." In addition, Nvidia will also invest $2 billion to support R&D and fab. expansion for its U.S.-based manufacturing facilities.Considering the number of bottlenecks that have appeared since the memory supply shortage began in 2025, from glass cloth to PCB drill bits, Nvidia's investment in expanding photonics development and fabrication in America may be a way to get ahead of future supply constraints, too.Hedging its bets (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)With the similarities between these two investment vehicles for Nvidia, and the near identical nature of the deals and subsequent press releases, it very much feels like Nvidia is hedging its bets. Arguably, that's what Nvidia has been doing since the advent of this major AI revolution.Nvidia is the one major company making money hand over fist in this new era, and instead of hoarding it, or using it exclusively for stock buybacks, or corporate bonuses, it's been reinvesting in the AI industry that put a rocket up its balance sheets and stock price. You may like Nvidia's $20 billion Groq IP deal bolsters AI market domination OpenAI raises $110 billion in largest-ever private tech funding round, Nvidia throws in $30 billion Nvidia's plan to invest $100 billion in OpenAI appears unlikely It invested in Coreweave, OpenAI, Oracle, Intel, Synopsys, and Nokia, all of which are heavily involved in the AI industry or are adjacent to it. It's keenly aware that even as the king of the AI pile, it needs those other economic and industry pillars to keep the whole house of cards standing.If Nvidia were bold enough, it could try to buy out Lumentum and Coherent entirely. Their company valuations are a fraction of Nvidia's profits from even the past quarter. But it didn't. It secured capacity, but not exclusive rights. Nvidia isn't looking to eat the AI industry and supply chain, but bolster its output.It's not a one horse raceAs much as Nvidia is the clear market leader when it comes to AI hardware, it is far from the only player in the game. China has used Nvidia's absence to heavily invest and fuel its domestic inference chip industry. AMD recently signed a $60 billion deal with Meta to power its next-generation AI-powering data centers, and there are small startup companies looking to build out ASIC chips that could prove far more powerful for future AI workloads when fully developed.Marvell's Celestial AI acquisition highlights that other companies see optical technologies as a key future technology for next-generation networking hardware. Meanwhile, the Bill Gates-backed Neurophos is developing AI chips that use photonics to accelerate even beyond what the best GPUs can do.Nvidia made itself the king of the AI hill by having the best hardware at the right time, and as far as training goes, that doesn't seem likely to change any time soon. But if Nvidia doesn't keep developing its hardware, other contenders would emerge. And the competition is ramping up in the inference space, leaving no guarantee that Nvidia will retain its premier position forever.Perhaps getting ahead of the pack when it comes to photonics will be one way it can maintain its lead. But even if it doesn't, $4 billion is a drop in the bucket for a company that made close to 20 times that in the past three months alone.
items.author: Jon Martindale
items.titleZh: 英伟达投资40亿美元强化光子学产业——注资Lumentum与Coherent支持美国研发制造，确保数据中心互联供应链产能与未来供应
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达近日宣布向两家光电子制造企业Lumentum和Coherent各投资20亿美元，总额达40亿美元。这一举措延续了英伟达利用其充裕现金流进行资本投资、构建AI生态系统的战略，同时也旨在通过前瞻性技术布局保持其在AI硬件领域的领先地位。

两家被投公司均专注于光学与半导体技术，致力于开发利用光而非电信号传输数据的未来网络硬件与AI芯片。随着AI引发历史上最大规模的计算基础设施建设，传统电子传输在带宽和能耗方面逐渐面临瓶颈。光子技术通过激光在光波导中传输数据，具有高带宽、低延迟和低功耗的潜力，被视为突破当前AI算力限制的关键路径。

英伟达首席执行官黄仁勋表示，公司将与Lumentum共同推动全球最先进的硅光子技术，以建设下一代千兆瓦规模的“AI工厂”。Coherent首席执行官Jim Anderson则指出，此次合作将强化其作为下一代AI数据中心基础设施关键赋能者的角色。

具体投资结构包含多重维度：除了直接注入资金支持两家公司在美国的研发与制造扩张（包括新建晶圆厂），英伟达还作出了“数十亿美元的采购承诺”，并获得了“先进激光组件及光网络产品的未来产能获取权”。这种“现金+订单+产能锁定”的模式，既保障了英伟达自身未来对关键光子元件的供应链安全，也有助于应对自2025年内存短缺以来从玻璃布到PCB钻头等一系列产业链瓶颈。

从战略角度看，英伟达对两家业务高度重叠的公司进行平行投资，显示出其在技术路线上的风险对冲意图。尽管英伟达完全有能力收购这些规模相对较小的企业，但其选择了非排他性的合作模式，旨在强化而非吞并整个AI供应链。这与英伟达近期对CoreWeave、OpenAI、英特尔等生态伙伴的一系列投资逻辑一脉相承——通过滋养整个AI产业生态来巩固自身的核心地位。

当前AI硬件领域虽由英伟达主导，但竞争正日趋激烈。中国在英伟达芯片受限的背景下大力扶持本土推理芯片产业；AMD与Meta签署了价值600亿美元的AI数据中心芯片协议；Marvell通过收购Celestial AI加码光学互联技术；由比尔·盖茨支持的Neurophos等初创公司也在研发基于光子学的AI芯片，试图实现比现有GPU更强的性能突破。

尽管在AI训练领域英伟达仍占据绝对优势，但在推理市场和其他新兴架构的挑战下，其长期领先地位并非高枕无忧。提前布局光子学这类变革性技术，是英伟达维持竞争优势的重要战略落子。即使未来技术路线发生变化，这笔投资对一家单季利润即超百亿美元的公司而言风险可控，却可能为其赢得关键的未来技术窗口期。

（本文基于Tom's Hardware报道分析生成）

The combo deal saves you a tidy $236.99 off the list price of $1346.98. If you're going to use the fastest gaming processor on the market for a high-spec gaming PC, it's good to pair it with a premium, feature-rich motherboard. Grab this deal at Newegg We've reviewed the Asus ROG Strix X870E-E Gaming WiFi motherboard, awarding it 4 out of 5 stars and touting it as one of the best motherboard options in the $500 bracket. This mobo actually comes with three PCIe 5.0 M.2 sockets, a further two PCIe 4.0 M.2 sockets, and an abundance of precious USB ports. Excellent voltage regulation and 18+2+2 power solution rated for 110A per stage.Corsair's Vengeance RAM is also a very popular brand name and a popular choice for memory in the PC building community. This kit offers 2x 16GB paired sticks for a total of 32GB of DDR5 RAM at 6400MT/s, a sweet spot speed for AMD processors on the AM5 platform. In the current climate, this RAM is priced at $437, but combining it with the processor and motherboard here gets you the same kit for the relative price of just $201. items.titleZh: 搭配AMD新款9850X3D处理器与X870E主板，仅需201美元即可拥有32GB海盗船复仇者内存——以1,109美元打造炫酷AM5主机。
items.descriptionZh:
知名硬件零售商Newegg日前推出了一项极具吸引力的硬件捆绑促销活动，将构建一台高性能游戏PC的核心组件组合出售，为DIY装机爱好者提供了一个高性价比的解决方案。

此次促销的核心是一个三件套组合，包括：AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D处理器、华硕ROG Strix X870E-E Gaming WiFi主板以及海盗船Vengeance RGB 32GB（2x16GB）DDR5-6400内存套装。该捆绑包的售价为1109.99美元，相比这三件商品单独购买的标价总和1346.98美元，直接为消费者节省了236.99美元，折扣幅度约为18%。

**组件详细解析与技术亮点：**

1.  **AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D处理器：** 作为该捆绑包的核心，这款处理器基于AM5平台，拥有8个核心。新闻明确指出，凭借略微提升的功耗设定，它已取代Ryzen 7 9800X3D，成为当前市场上游戏性能最快的处理器。其关键创新在于延续了AMD备受赞誉的3D V-Cache技术，通过堆叠超大容量的L3缓存，显著提升了众多游戏的表现，特别是在需要频繁数据交换的场景中能带来更高的帧率。

2.  **华硕ROG Strix X870E-E Gaming WiFi主板：** 这款高端主板是搭配旗舰处理器的理想选择。它获得了Tom's Hardware 4星（满分5星）的评价，被认为是500美元价位段的优秀产品。其主要技术优势包括：**丰富的扩展性**——提供了多达三个PCIe 5.0 M.2 SSD插槽和两个PCIe 4.0 M.2插槽，为未来高速存储升级预留了充足空间；**强大的供电设计**——采用了18+2+2相供电方案，每相可承载110A电流，确保了即使在高负载下也能为处理器提供稳定、纯净的电力；**充裕的连接端口**——配备了大量的USB接口，满足外设扩展需求。

3.  **海盗船Vengeance RGB DDR5-6400内存：** 该内存套装容量为32GB（双通道），运行频率为6400 MT/s。新闻强调，这个频率对于AM5平台的AMD处理器而言是一个“甜点”速度，能在性能、稳定性和兼容性之间取得最佳平衡。海盗船品牌在DIY社区中享有很高的声誉。

**性能与市场背景分析：**
报道通过性能对比指出，Ryzen 7 9850X3D在游戏基准测试中与其前代产品9800X3D成绩接近，但仍能保持微弱的领先优势。这巩固了其作为顶级游戏CPU的地位。

此外，新闻特别指出了当前（指新闻发布时所处的“2026年”市场环境）一个严峻的背景：**内存价格正处在异常高昂的时期**。文中提到，该内存套装单独售价高达437美元，而在此捆绑包中，其折算成本仅为201美元。这反映了全球内存市场供应紧张或成本上涨给PC爱好者带来的财务压力，并建议用户若需购买内存应尽早行动。在此背景下，该捆绑包的性价比尤为突出。

**附加价值与应用场景：**
购买此捆绑包的用户还将获得两份免费赠品：价值55.99美元的华硕TUF Gaming M4 Air轻量化游戏鼠标，以及一份价值70美元的游戏《赤色沙漠》（Crimson Desert）。这进一步提升了整个套餐的附加值。

**总结：**
该促销活动精准定位于追求顶级游戏性能的PC装机用户。它将市场上领先的游戏CPU、扩展性与供电俱佳的高端主板，以及处于“甜点”频率的知名品牌大容量内存相结合，并在内存价格高企的市场环境下，通过捆绑销售提供了显著的价格优惠和额外的游戏外设赠品，构成了一个构建高性能游戏主机的完整、高效且具有成本效益的核心解决方案。

items.title: Micron sampling first 256GB SOCAMM2 memory packages to customers — 2TB of RAM per CPU is now in reach of datacenter players
items.description: Micron is now sampling 256GB SOCAMM2 units, enabling capacities of up to 2TB of LPDDR5X per CPU in AI servers.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Most of the conversation about speed in AI datacenters revolves around the accelerators themselves, discussing tokens per second and the like. However, the battle for AI performance is fought on multiple fronts, and one of them is in memory capacity and power efficiency. Today, Micron unveiled what look to be the industry's first 256 GB SOCAMM2 units, a sizable step up from the last-best 192 GB modules released just six months ago.Go deeper with TH Premium: Memory (Image credit: SK Hynix)AI data centers are swallowing the world's memory and storage supplyThe future of DRAM: From DDR5 to future ICsHigh-bandwidth memory roadmapHere's why HBM is coming for your PC's RAMThe company says it's now shipping samples to customers, who will most definitely be happy at the notion of having 2TB of memory wired to each CPU. For just one major example, the typical Nvidia NVL72 rack can now carry 72 TB of RAM for its 36 CPUs.The 33% improved density over previous-generation SOCAMM2 is excellent news on its own, but that's not the only advantage of this form factor. The new modules ought to offer 66% better power efficiency compared to bog-standard RDIMMs, and they're compatible with increasingly popular (and necessary) liquid cooling for AI servers. You may like Samsung introduces SOCAMM2 LPDDR5X memory module for AI data centers Intel-certified 256 GB DDR5 stick could cut Xeon memory power by 18%, saving millions of dollars Here's why HBM is coming for your PC's RAM According to Micron, the new sticks are the first to employ its 32 Gb (4 GB) LPDDR5X monolithic dies, where "monolithic" means all the memory and relevant circuitry are part of a single die. (Image credit: Micron)Given the target market for these large SOCAMM2s, the firm touts the real-world performance improvements beyond just density and power efficiency. Having this much RAM available to a single processor lets AI models use much larger context windows. Consequently, it helps reduce the all-important TTFT (Time To First Token), meaning bots start answering your questions quicker.In an AI future where context is literally everything, every gigabyte of memory closer to the xPUs in a system matters, and Micron's advancement today will doubtless be found in massive AI server installations worldwide as companies allocate hundreds of billions of dollars of capex in the race toward AI supremacy.The SOCAMM2 form factor is the result of a partnership between Nvidia and memory makers Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. The SOCAMM standard was originally designed by Nvidia, but the accelerator mogul reportedly had trouble getting the modules to operate without overheating on high-density servers. CEO Jensen Huang wisely teamed up with the folks who make computer memory for a living, resulting in SOCAMM2s with growing density and lower power consumption.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Bruno Ferreira
items.titleZh: 美光率先向客户提供256GB SOCAMM2内存模组样品——数据中心运营商即将实现单CPU 2TB内存
items.descriptionZh:
在人工智能数据中心领域，性能竞争不仅局限于计算加速器本身的内存带宽与算力指标，更延伸至内存容量与能效等关键战场。近日，美光（Micron）正式发布了业界首款容量达256GB的SOCAMM2内存模组，标志着服务器内存技术取得重大突破。该模组距离上一代最高192GB容量的产品发布仅过去六个月，密度实现了33%的显著提升。

此次发布的核心创新在于模组采用了美光新型的32Gb（4GB）单片LPDDR5X内存芯片。所谓“单片”，是指所有存储单元及相关控制电路均集成于单一芯片内，这种设计有助于提升集成度、优化信号完整性与能效。新产品已开始向客户送样，其高密度特性使得单颗CPU可支持的内存容量高达2TB。以典型的NVIDIA NVL72机架为例，其36颗CPU现在可搭载总计72TB的内存。

除了容量飞跃，SOCAMM2形态在能效和散热方面优势突出。相比传统的RDIMM（寄存式双列直插内存模组），新款模组的能效提升高达66%，并且兼容日益成为AI服务器标配的液冷散热方案。这对于功耗巨大的AI数据中心而言，意味着可观的运营成本节约与散热设计简化。

对于AI应用的实际性能，大容量内存带来的直接益处是支持更大的模型上下文窗口。这能有效缩短生成式AI响应请求的“首词元时间”（TTFT），即用户获得AI答复的初始延迟显著降低。在AI推理与训练中，更靠近处理器（xPU）的每一GB内存都至关重要，美光此次技术进步将直接赋能全球各大AI超算集群的建设。

值得注意的是，SOCAMM2标准本身是产业链协同创新的成果。该外形规格源于NVIDIA与美光、三星、SK海力士等头部内存制造商的合作。最初由NVIDIA设计的SOCAMM标准在高密度服务器上面临过热挑战，促使CEO黄仁勋联合专业内存制造商共同优化，最终催生了在密度与能效上持续迭代的SOCAMM2方案。随着全球企业在AI领域投入数千亿美元资本支出，此类高效能内存模组将成为支撑AI算力基础设施演进的核心组件之一。

items.title: Gemini API key thief racks up $82,314 in charges in just two days, victim 'facing bankruptcy' — affected devs call for basic guardrails against 'catastrophic usage anomalies'
items.description: A Google Gemini user has taken to Reddit 'in a state of shock and panic' after getting an $82,314 bill.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter A Google Gemini user has taken to Reddit “in a state of shock and panic.” The issue is with the most recent bill received by their software development business. Redditor RatonVaquero’s typical monthly spend on Gemini AI services is $180. However, in just 48 hours last month, their account “generated $82,314.44 in charges.” A thief has been using the account to generate oodles of Gemini 3 Pro Images and Texts. If Google doesn’t back down regarding these non-trivial fees from the suspected “stolen Gemini API key,” it will bankrupt the company.Tragically, locking the door after the horse has bolted, RatonVaquero has now “Deleted the compromised key, Disabled Gemini APIs, Rotated credentials, Enabled 2FA everywhere, Locked down IAM, [and] Opened a support case.” On the latter point, initial feedback from a Google rep they contacted indicates that the charges will probably stick.From the Redditor’s discussion of their correspondence with Google so far, it looks like the “don’t be evil” company is going to repeatedly cite its ‘Shared Responsibility Model’ for cloud services accounts. I’ve had a quick look at the referenced legal word salad, and I’d guess Google is leaning on the part of its agreement that asks customers to have an authentication system, access policy, and network security in place to protect their API keys, among other things. You may like Google reports that state hackers from China, Russia and Iran are using Gemini in 'all stages' of attacks Alphabet is doubling its capital expenditure to a staggering $180 billion in 2026 AI.com's $85 million Super Bowl ad campaign falls foul as traffic crashes servers Interestingly, though, several Redditors also note that the stolen API key(s) might actually have been there for the taking, and it is Google’s fault for flipping its API key secrecy rules. (Image credit: Google Gemini)Arguing for some ‘mercy,’ RatonVaquero, one of three devs at the affected Mexican development firm, complains that Google doesn’t have “basic guardrails for catastrophic usage anomalies.” The contrast in usage, from a usual $180pcm to $82,000+ in 48 hours, does indeed look like an extreme spike. RatonVaquero also says that there should be features like temporarily freezing services until review and the implementation of per-API spending caps.A look into this overcharging issue indicates that Personal/consumer Gemini customers can’t accidentally spend more than their flat monthly fee. Instead, they have usage caps. Moving up to Dev/Business Google AI Studio users, they can set Quotas (limiting the number of requests per day or per minute). Meanwhile, Google Cloud (Vertex AI) users can set Budget Alerts to notify them when they reach a certain dollar amount.RatonVaquero says they will talk again with a Google rep soon, and have filed a cybercrime report with the FBI. Now they are basically hoping for a softening of big G’s stance. They may be able to share the logs of their unusual “455x spike” in usage, and ask for “goodwill credits” as victims of a cybersecurity incident. It is Kafkaesque, but usually a bit of stubborn persistence can help get your case seen by the right people for a more favorable outcome.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Mark Tyson
items.titleZh: Gemini API密钥被盗两天狂刷8.2万美元，受害者"面临破产"——开发者呼吁设置基本防护栏应对"灾难性异常使用"
items.descriptionZh:
一名谷歌Gemini用户在Reddit上发帖，表示自己“处于震惊和恐慌之中”。问题出在其软件开发公司收到的最新账单上。用户RatonVaquero通常每月在Gemini AI服务上的花费约为180美元。然而，就在上个月的短短48小时内，其账户“产生了82,314.44美元的费用”。这是一名盗贼利用其账户生成了海量的Gemini 3 Pro图像和文本内容。如果谷歌不就这笔来自疑似“被盗Gemini API密钥”的巨额费用让步，将导致该公司破产。

可悲的是，亡羊补牢为时已晚。RatonVaquero现已采取一系列补救措施：“删除了被盗的密钥、禁用了Gemini API、更新了凭证、在所有地方启用了双重验证（2FA）、锁定了身份和访问管理（IAM）、并提交了支持案例”。然而，就最后一点而言，从其联系的谷歌代表处获得的初步反馈表明，这笔费用很可能需要照付。

从该Reddit用户对目前与谷歌沟通情况的描述来看，这家标榜“不作恶”的公司可能会反复引用其云服务账户的“责任共担模型”。快速浏览相关法律条款后可以推断，谷歌很可能依据协议中要求客户建立身份验证系统、访问策略和网络安全措施以保护其API密钥等内容作为依据。

但有趣的是，一些Reddit用户也指出，被盗的API密钥可能本身就被轻易获取，而这要归咎于谷歌改变了其API密钥的保密规则。

作为受影响墨西哥软件开发公司的三名开发者之一，RatonVaquero请求谷歌施以“仁慈”，并抱怨谷歌没有为“灾难性的使用异常设置基本的安全护栏”。使用量从通常的每月180美元激增至48小时内超过82,000美元，这确实是一个极端的峰值。RatonVaquero还表示，谷歌应该提供诸如暂时冻结服务以待审查，以及为每个API设置消费上限等功能。

深入探讨此次超额收费问题可以发现，个人/消费者类Gemini用户不可能意外花费超过其固定的月费，因为他们有使用量上限。对于开发/商业版的Google AI Studio用户，他们可以设置配额（限制每天或每分钟的请求数量）。而Google Cloud（Vertex AI）用户可以设置预算提醒，在达到特定金额时获得通知。

RatonVaquero表示将很快再次与谷歌代表沟通，并已向FBI提交了网络犯罪报告。目前，他们基本上寄希望于谷歌的态度能有所软化。他们或许能够提供显示其使用量出现“455倍激增”的异常日志，并作为网络安全事件的受害者申请“善意抵扣”。这个过程虽然堪称卡夫卡式的荒诞，但通常坚持不懈的努力有助于让合适的人看到你的案例，从而获得更有利的结果。

**事件核心问题分析：**
此次事件突显了几个关键问题：
1.  **API密钥安全管理**：API密钥是访问云端AI服务的凭证，一旦泄露，攻击者即可无限制地滥用，产生巨额费用。用户（尤其是中小型企业）对密钥的保护意识和能力可能存在不足，而云服务提供商的安全策略变更也可能引入风险。
2.  **消费控制与异常监测机制缺失**：在面向开发者和企业的灵活计费模式下，缺乏强制性的、硬性的消费上限设置。虽然提供了配额和警报，但这些属于可选配置，且警报仅为事后通知，无法主动阻止异常消费的发生。服务提供商缺乏对异常使用模式（如短时间内费用激增数百倍）进行自动识别和干预的“熔断”机制。
3.  **责任界定模糊**：云服务商的“责任共担模型”将密钥保护的主要责任置于用户端。但当发生明显异常、可能导致用户破产的恶意消费时，服务商是否应承担部分风险管控失察的责任，或在处理此类事件时展现更大的灵活性，引发了争议。

**潜在影响与行业启示：**
1.  **对开发者的警示**：开发者必须将API密钥视为最高机密，实施严格的访问控制（如最小权限原则）、启用所有可用的安全功能（如2FA），并定期轮换密钥。不能完全依赖服务商的基础保护。
2.  **对云服务商的要求**：此事件可能会促使谷歌及其他云/AI服务提供商（如AWS、Azure、OpenAI等）重新评估其计费安全策略。未来可能会推出更严格的默认消费限制（尤其是对新账户或中小额度账户），增强实时异常检测与自动干预能力，并明确针对“明显欺诈或盗用所致超额费用”的申诉与减免流程。
3.  **行业最佳实践**：预计将推动在开发者社区和中小企业中，更广泛地采用“零信任”安全架构、专用的密钥管理服务以及更精细化的云成本监控与管理工具。

此次事件不仅是单个用户的财务危机，更是对整个基于API的云消费模式安全性的一次拷问，可能加速相关防护措施和行业规范的完善。

items.titleZh: 揭秘：导致人工智能政治抵抗运动的秘密会议内幕
items.descriptionZh:
今年1月初，约90位政治、社区与思想领袖聚集在新奥尔良万豪酒店，参加一场关于人工智能的秘密会议。会议保密程度极高，所有受邀者在踏入会场前均不知晓其他参会者身份。

会议现场呈现出罕见景象：教会领袖与保守派学者邻座，工会代表与科技伦理学家并肩。曾助力伯尼·桑德斯竞选的进步派人士，竟与特朗普支持者呼吸同一片空气。会议发起人——几位AI思想领袖——在紧张氛围中暗自祈祷这场“意识形态熔炉”不会爆发冲突。

这场代号“生命未来”的闭门会议揭示了一个迫切现实：尽管美国社会陷入深度分裂，但跨越政治光谱的群体正形成罕见共识——人工智能将重塑就业、教育、医疗乃至民主制度本身。参会者从基督教广播员到硅谷批判者，从工会组织者到前总统顾问，共同忧虑着AI可能加剧社会不平等、侵蚀人类自主性。

会议核心讨论聚焦三大矛盾：技术乐观主义与人文主义的冲突，算法权力集中化与民主监督的张力，以及AI创新速度与社会适应能力的断层。值得注意的是，极左与极右阵营在“警惕科技寡头垄断”和“保障人类尊严”问题上意外达成共识，这或为未来AI治理的跨党派合作埋下伏笔。

这场秘密集会标志着AI伦理讨论已突破科技圈层，进入更广阔的社会协商阶段。当自由派教师工会领袖与保守派活动家开始使用相同术语讨论算法偏见，当基督教伦理学家与无神论科学家共同起草AI宪章准则，或许暗示着人类社会正在摸索应对技术奇点的新型对话机制。

尽管会议未发布正式宣言，但其象征意义深远：在AI可能重构文明基石的历史节点，传统意识形态对立正让位于更根本的“人类集体生存”议题。这场新奥尔良的密室对话，或许正在悄然绘制未来人机共存时代的权力地图。

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel late on Tuesday announced that its board of directors had elected Dr. Craig H. Barratt as independent chairman, who will assume his role in mid-May after the company's Annual Stockholders’ Meeting. Craig H. Barratt, who has an engineering background, will replace Frank D. Yeary, who will retire from Intel's BoD after spending around 17 years there.Tom's Hardware Premium Roadmaps (Image credit: Future)High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) Roadmap Nvidia Enterprise GPU and CPU RoadmapAI accelerator RoadmapDesktop GPU Roadmap3D NAND RoadmapBarrat replaces Frank D. Yeary, who has a financial background and who once tried to split Intel into products and manufacturing companies and then get rid of the company's manufacturing assets. Instead of splitting Intel, Craig H. Barratt seems to envision the company as an integrated devices manufacturer with rigorous execution."The company has taken significant steps to strengthen its financial position, advance its technology and product roadmap, and enhance operational discipline," said Barratt. "The board thanks Frank for his leadership and for helping position Intel for this next phase. I am honored to lead the board's continued focus on supporting rigorous execution, investing in and scaling U.S.-anchored R&D and manufacturing, and ensuring Intel is well positioned to compete and win in the years ahead." You may like Intel Foundry boss leaves for Qualcomm Intel appoints Trump economic advisor as head of government affairs Eric Demers leaves for Intel after 14 years at Qualcomm Barratt is a longtime semiconductor executive and entrepreneur with more than three decades of experience spanning wireless connectivity and networking silicon while working at Intel and Qualcomm. Over the course of his career, he has worked across both startups and major technology companies and has plenty of experience in leading engineering-centric organizations and bringing complex silicon products to market.Barratt is best known for leading Atheros Communications, a leading supplier of Wi-Fi processors for PCs, networking gear, and consumer electronics. Under his leadership, the firm expanded rapidly during the early wireless-networking boom, went public in 2004, and was eventually acquired by Qualcomm for roughly $3.1 billion. Following the acquisition, Craig H. Barratt served as president of Qualcomm Atheros, where he was in charge of connectivity silicon used in a wide range of devices from smartphones and PCs to routers and switches.After leaving Qualcomm, Craig H. Barratt worked as Senior Vice President, Access and Energy, at Google from 2012 until 2017. Later on, he briefly led Barefoot Networks, a startup developing programmable Ethernet switching processors aimed at gamers that Intel acquired in 2019, and afterward oversaw Intel's Ethernet and networking businesses that were part of Intel's Data Platforms Group.Barratt returned to Intel in November 2025 by joining the board as an independent director. Barratt serves as a director at companies such as Astera Labs and Intuitive Surgical, which provide him exposure to both data center infrastructure and advanced robotics systems.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsBarratt earned a Ph.D. in electrical engineering from Stanford University after completing earlier studies in Australia."With a stronger balance sheet, meaningful progress across our roadmap — including Intel 18A and 14A — and a clear path forward under Lip-Bu, this is the appropriate time for me to step down as chair and from the board and transition leadership to a new independent chair,” said Yeary. items.titleZh: 英特尔董事长弗兰克·耶里卸任，克雷格·巴雷特将出任董事会新主席
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔董事会于本周二晚间宣布，选举克雷格·H·巴雷特博士（Dr. Craig H. Barratt）为独立董事长，他将在公司年度股东大会后的五月中旬正式就任。巴雷特拥有工程背景，将接替已在英特尔董事会任职约17年、具有金融背景的弗兰克·D·耶里（Frank D. Yeary）。耶里此前曾试图推动英特尔分拆为产品与制造两家公司，并剥离其制造资产。而巴雷特的接任，似乎预示着英特尔将继续坚持集成设备制造商（IDM）的战略定位，并强调严格的执行。

巴雷特在声明中表示：“公司已采取重要措施来强化其财务状况、推进技术及产品路线图，并提升运营纪律。董事会感谢弗兰克的领导及其为英特尔下一阶段发展所做的铺垫。我很荣幸能够领导董事会持续聚焦于支持严谨的执行、投资并扩展以美国为锚点的研发与制造，确保英特尔在未来数年具备竞争力并赢得市场。”

巴雷特是一位资深的半导体行业高管和创业者，拥有超过三十年的无线连接与网络芯片领域经验，曾任职于英特尔和高通。他的职业生涯横跨初创公司与大型科技企业，在领导以工程为核心的组织以及推动复杂芯片产品市场化方面经验丰富。他最广为人知的成就是领导了Atheros通信公司——一家为PC、网络设备和消费电子提供Wi-Fi处理器的领先供应商。在其带领下，该公司在早期无线网络热潮中迅速扩张，于2004年上市，并最终被高通以约31亿美元收购。收购后，巴雷特担任高通Atheros总裁，负责用于智能手机、PC、路由器及交换机等广泛设备的连接芯片业务。

离开高通后，巴雷特于2012年至2017年在谷歌担任接入与能源高级副总裁。随后，他短暂领导过Barefoot Networks——一家专注于为游戏玩家开发可编程以太网交换处理器的初创公司，该公司于2019年被英特尔收购。此后，他负责监管英特尔数据平台集团旗下的以太网及网络业务。

巴雷特于2025年11月重返英特尔，以独立董事身份加入董事会。他目前还在Astera Labs和Intuitive Surgical等公司担任董事，这使他在数据中心基础设施和先进机器人系统领域均有所涉猎。巴雷特在澳大利亚完成早期学业后，于斯坦福大学获得了电气工程博士学位。

耶里在离任时表示：“随着资产负债表更加健康、我们的路线图（包括Intel 18A和14A）取得显著进展，以及在李保国领导下路径清晰，现在是我卸任董事长及董事会职务、将领导权过渡给新任独立董事长的合适时机。”

此次人事变动标志着英特尔董事会领导层从财务导向向工程与技术背景的转变，呼应了公司当前强化技术执行、聚焦先进制程研发与本土制造的战略重心。巴雷特的深厚技术背景及其在连接与网络芯片领域的成功经验，有望助力英特尔在数据中心、人工智能及网络基础设施等关键增长领域加速创新与市场拓展。

items.title: Seagate begins shipping 44TB hard drives with HAMR tech to data centers — Mozaic 4+ platform expands to 10 platters
items.description: Seagate has begun shipping its 44TB Exos HDDs based on its HAMR technology to two hyperscale customers as others qualify the all-new Mozaic 4+ hard drive platform.

Subscribe to our newsletter Seagate has announced that it has begun shipments of its latest hard disk drives featuring a 44TB capacity to two of its partners among the leading hyperscale cloud service providers (CSPs). The new HDDs are based on the company's Mozaic 4+ platform that relies on Seagate's heat-assisted magnetic recording (HAMR) technology.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowSeagate's Mozaic 4+ platform uses 10 platters with up to 4+ TB capacity per platter to enable drives of up to 44TB. The drives are believed to feature a 7200 RPM spindle speed as well as conventional magnetic recording (CMR) and therefore offer predictably high performance by HDD standards: think sustained data transfer rates of around 300 MB/s, though we are speculating.Normally, Seagate and other makers of HDDs tend to release a lineup of hard drives offering different capacity points upon formal launch. With its Mozaic 4+ platform, Seagate is changing its tactics and only formally introduce its 44TB HDD that is currently being shipped to just one hyperscaler. Other large CSPs are currently qualifying Mozaic 4+-based drives, though we do not know which capacity points we are talking about.Perhaps, the main reason why Seagate disclosed shipments of its 44TB HDDs now is the fact that its arch-rival Western Digital announced plans to ship its 40TB hard drives in the second half of the year. Unlike Seagate's Exos 44TB HDDs, Western Digital's 40TB drive does not rely on HAMR technology. Instead, it uses energy-assisted magnetic recording (EAMR) and UltraSMR technology that greatly expands capacity at the cost of slow overwrite performance. By making this announcement now, Seagate sends its potential customers a clear message that its HAMR-based hard 44TB drives are more technologically advanced than WD's 40TB offerings. You may like Seagate updates its lineups with new 32 TB CMR hard drives, starting at $699.99 Western Digital unveils massive 40TB HDD with energy-assisted recording tech Western Digital details 14-platter 3.5-inch HAMR HDD designs with 140 TB and beyond While the difference between 40TB and 44TB may seem immaterial, when deployed at scale, that difference may become significant when power consumption per TB is considered. Seagate compares its 44TB offering to 30TB drives and claims that the new drives increase overall system efficiency by roughly 47%, which enables hyperscalers to shrink physical footprint of their storage clusters by around 100 square feet while trimming annual power consumption by approximately 0.8 million kilowatt-hours."Data has become one of the most valuable assets for enterprises, fueling business insights, enhancing productivity, and enabling competitive advantage," said Dave Mosley, Seagate's chair and chief executive officer. "As the foundation of modern data center infrastructure, data storage solutions are essential to manage ever-increasing data volumes and maximize returns on investments in today's AI driven-world. Seagate's HAMR-based Mozaic products deliver the scale, performance, and efficiency customers need to unlock the full potential of their data" Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors

items.titleZh: 希捷开始向数据中心供货44TB HAMR硬盘——Mozaic 4+平台拓展至10个盘片，存储密度翻倍
items.descriptionZh:
希捷公司宣布，已开始向两家领先的超大规模云服务提供商合作伙伴发货其最新款容量达44TB的硬盘驱动器。这些新硬盘基于公司的Mozaic 4+平台，该平台依赖于希捷的热辅助磁记录技术。

Mozaic 4+平台采用10张盘片，每张盘片容量超过4TB，从而实现了高达44TB的总容量。据悉，这些驱动器具备7200 RPM的主轴转速，并采用传统磁记录技术，因此以硬盘标准来看，能提供可预期的高性能，持续数据传输速率预计约为300 MB/s。

通常，希捷和其他硬盘制造商在正式发布时会推出一系列不同容量的硬盘产品线。但凭借Mozaic 4+平台，希捷改变了策略，目前仅正式推出44TB硬盘，并且仅发货给一家超大规模云服务商。其他大型云服务提供商目前正在对基于Mozaic 4+的驱动器进行资格认证，不过具体涉及哪些容量点尚不明确。

希捷选择此刻披露其44TB硬盘的发货情况，一个主要原因是其竞争对手西部数据宣布计划在今年下半年发货40TB硬盘。与希捷的Exos 44TB硬盘不同，西部数据的40TB驱动器并未采用HAMR技术，而是使用了能量辅助磁记录技术和UltraSMR技术，后者以较慢的覆写性能为代价极大地扩展了容量。希捷通过此公告向潜在客户清晰地传达了其基于HAMR的44TB硬盘在技术上比西部数据的40TB产品更先进的信息。

虽然40TB和44TB的容量差异看似不大，但在大规模部署时，考虑到每TB的功耗，这一差异可能变得显著。希捷将其44TB产品与30TB驱动器进行比较，声称新款驱动器将整体系统效率提升了约47%，这使得超大规模运营商能够将其存储集群的物理占地面积减少约100平方英尺，同时每年减少约80万千瓦时的功耗。

希捷董事长兼首席执行官戴夫·莫斯利表示：“数据已成为企业最宝贵的资产之一，它能推动商业洞察、提升生产力并带来竞争优势。作为现代数据中心基础设施的基石，数据存储解决方案对于管理不断增长的数据量及在当今人工智能驱动的世界中最大化投资回报至关重要。希捷基于HAMR的Mozaic产品提供了客户所需的规模、性能和效率，以释放其数据的全部潜力。”

**核心技术原理与创新点**：
- **热辅助磁记录**：HAMR技术通过使用激光瞬态加热磁盘介质上的微小区域，降低其矫顽力，从而允许磁头在更高密度的区域写入数据。这是实现每盘片超过4TB超高面密度的关键。
- **Mozaic 4+平台**：该平台整合了HAMR技术、新一代介质、全新写入与读取磁头以及先进的电子控制系统。其创新在于成功将10张超高容量盘片集成到标准3.5英寸外形规格中。
- **与传统及替代技术对比**：
    - **与传统CMR**：相比普通传统磁记录硬盘，HAMR实现了容量上的巨大飞跃。
    - **与西部数据EAMR+UltraSMR**：HAMR被认为代表了下一代磁记录技术的发展方向，而EAMR（能量辅助磁记录，如微波辅助记录MAMR）和UltraSMR（叠瓦式磁记录的强化版）则是当前阶段的高容量方案。SMR技术在顺序写入时性能尚可，但随机覆写性能较差，适用于冷存储或顺序写入负载。希捷强调其44TB硬盘采用CMR，意味着在随机写入性能上预计优于基于SMR的方案。

**性能参数与影响分析**：
- **容量与密度**：44TB容量，单盘片超4TB，均为行业领先水平。
- **能效与空间效率**：相较于30TB硬盘，可提升约47%的系统效率（以每瓦特性能或每单位空间的容量计），这对电力成本高昂、空间有限的大型数据中心极具吸引力。
- **市场与战略意义**：此次发货标志着HAMR技术经过多年研发后，正式进入大规模商业部署阶段。希捷意在抢占技术制高点，巩固其在高端企业级硬盘市场的领导地位，特别是在AI浪潮驱动数据中心存储需求激增的背景下。

**应用场景**：
新款44TB硬盘主要面向超大规模云服务提供商和大型企业数据中心，适用于需要存储海量数据的场景，如：
- 人工智能与机器学习训练数据的存储
- 大规模云存储服务
- 数据备份与归档
- 媒体内容库
- 大数据分析存储池

总之，希捷44TB HAMR硬盘的出货是数据存储领域的一个重要里程碑，它不仅展示了HAMR技术的成熟，也为应对全球数据爆炸式增长，特别是AI驱动下的存储需求，提供了新的高容量、高密度解决方案。

items.title: Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption — suggests hyperscalers can reduce consumption as necessary, ensuring grid isn’t overloaded during peak demand
items.description: A U.K. study suggests that AI data centers can reduce their power demands during peak hours instead of running at maximum capacity at all times.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter A U.K. study run by the National Grid, Nvidia, Emerald AI, and the Electric Power Research Institute (EPRI) just showed that AI data centers do not need to run at peak demand at all times. According to Bloomberg, the report suggests that hyperscalers can quickly adjust their power consumption as needed, ensuring that the grid is not overloaded when other consumers increase demand, while also using excess power from renewable energy when demand falls. If AI tech companies agree to this arrangement, it could allow them to go online much sooner, especially as the lack of available grid power has been hampering the deployment of more AI GPUs.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThe system tested is very flexible and doesn't require much advanced scheduling. Testing showed that it took less than a minute for a data center operator to reduce the amount of power it used to around 66%, and that one data center could even run at just 10% capacity for 10 hours.This isn’t an ideal scenario for many hyperscalers, especially as they need as much power as they can get to maximize their expensive GPUs. However, insisting on this capability means that they’ll have to wait for the local grid to catch up (which can take years, if not decades), or they’ll have to deploy their own onsite generators (which can get expensive, unless they have deep pockets like OpenAI, and come with their own set of challenges). You may like U.S. electricity grid stretches thin as data centers rush to turn on onsite generators Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids This would be a win-win situation if both data centers and electricity grid operators could agree to run a system like this, as it would allow the former to expedite connecting their infrastructure to power while the latter could maximize their capacity even during off-peak hours. In fact, many utility companies already have a system that monitors spikes in demand and supercharges the grid as needed.There is a phenomenon called “The Great British Kettle Surge,” wherein U.K. power providers prepare the electricity grid for the expected surge during breaks on popular TV events like the World Cup. This happens because millions of households boil their kettles simultaneously during these breaks, resulting in a sudden peak in power demand for a few minutes.Now, instead of pouring in more electricity from power plants, which we’re already in short supply of, the operators could just ask AI data centers, which are one of, if not the biggest, power consumers, to reduce their demand. It might take some time and maybe some push from regulators before this can be implemented, but it’s a good short-term solution that will allow data centers to go online as soon as possible without breaking the grid. items.titleZh: 英伟达实验显示：AI数据中心可近乎实时灵活调节电力使用，这对全球能源消耗具有深远意义——超大规模运营商必要时可降低耗电量，确保电网在高峰需求期间不会过载。
items.descriptionZh:
由英国国家电网、英伟达、Emerald AI 和电力研究协会（EPRI）联合进行的一项研究表明，AI 数据中心无需始终以峰值需求运行。根据彭博社的报道，该报告指出，超大规模数据中心运营商可以快速按需调整其电力消耗，从而确保当其他电力消费者需求增加时电网不会过载，同时在需求下降时利用可再生能源产生的过剩电力。如果 AI 科技公司同意这种安排，将能够更快地使其数据中心上线，尤其是在当前电网可用电力不足已制约更多 AI GPU 部署的背景下。

测试的系统表现出高度灵活性，几乎不需要预先调度。测试结果显示，数据中心运营商能够在不到一分钟的时间内将其功耗降低至约 66%，其中一个数据中心甚至能以仅 10% 的容量持续运行 10 小时。然而，这对许多超大规模运营商来说并非理想方案，因为他们需要尽可能多的电力来最大化其昂贵 GPU 的利用率。但如果坚持要求获得全额稳定供电，则意味着他们必须等待当地电网升级（这可能需要数年甚至数十年），或者部署自己的现场发电机（除非像 OpenAI 那样资金雄厚，否则成本高昂，且会带来一系列新挑战）。

如果数据中心和电网运营商能够就运行此类系统达成一致，将创造双赢局面：前者可以加快其基础设施接入电网的速度，而后者则能在非高峰时段最大化利用电网容量。事实上，许多电力公司已经拥有监控需求激增并在需要时增强电网供电的系统。一个典型案例是“英国烧水壶高峰”现象：在英国，当世界杯等热门电视节目进入中场休息时，数百万家庭会同时烧水，导致电力需求在几分钟内出现尖峰。电力供应商会为此类预期中的用电高峰提前准备电网。

如今，面对已显短缺的电力供应，运营商可以转而请求 AI 数据中心（目前最大的电力消费者之一）降低其需求。虽然全面实施可能需要时间，甚至需要监管机构的推动，但这无疑是一个良好的短期解决方案，能让数据中心在不过度冲击电网的前提下尽快投入运营。

items.author: Elizabeth Lopatto
items.descriptionZh:
SpaceX潜在IPO计划背后的商业与理想碰撞

随着SpaceX被曝考虑启动首次公开募股（IPO），资本市场与航天领域同时掀起波澜。这一动向的特别之处在于，其创始人埃隆·马斯克长期以来对上市公司持鲜明批判态度，认为公开市场会阻碍长期战略性任务——尤其是火星殖民计划。

**背景与矛盾根源**
早在2013年，马斯克在内部邮件中明确表示：“我不愿将上市强加于SpaceX，特别是考虑到我们使命的长期性。”其传记作者阿什利·万斯披露，马斯克曾多次强调，在实现火星常态化飞行前上市将“损害公司核心目标”。这种立场源于上市公司需面临的季度财报压力、股东短期回报诉求与太空探索所需持续数十年的巨额投入之间的根本性冲突。

**当前动因：资金瓶颈与市场转向**
此次IPO传闻折射出私人资本市场对航天领域投资可能出现的饱和迹象。尽管SpaceX通过星链（Starlink）业务实现部分现金流，但火星殖民、星舰（Starship）研发及全球宽带网络部署仍需数千亿美元级投入。私募市场能否持续支撑如此规模的开支成为关键问题。与此同时，二级市场对“太空经济”概念的热情持续升温，可能促使公司重新评估融资路径。

**创新模式与潜在风险**
若SpaceX启动IPO，可能采用双重股权结构或特殊表决权设计，以确保马斯克团队保留对技术路线与战略决策的控制权。这种“技术独裁”模式在特斯拉已有实践，但在航天领域尚未验证。风险在于：1) 公众股东可能因长期亏损而提起诉讼；2) 太空任务失败可能导致股价剧烈波动，进而影响研发节奏；3) 信息披露要求或暴露关键技术细节。

**行业影响与场景推演**
若成功上市，SpaceX将成为首家以深空探索为核心业务的公众公司，重新定义“航天经济”的估值模型。短期看，募资可能加速星舰研发和月球基地建设；长期则需平衡火星愿景与股东回报。更深远的影响在于，这可能推动蓝色起源等竞争对手调整资本策略，进而重塑整个商业航天行业的竞争格局。

值得关注的是，马斯克曾在2021年表态：“当火星航行现金流可预测时，才会考虑SpaceX上市。”当前动向是否意味着星链已实现稳定收入，抑或是资本市场环境发生质变，将成为判断这场“理想主义与资本现实”博弈的关键坐标。

history.title: Taiwan expects power demand to increase by more than 5GW by 2030, enough to power nearly 4 million homes — rise in electricity consumption driven by semiconductor manufacturing and AI data center deployments
history.description: New semiconductor fabs and data centers are fueling Taiwan's growing power demand, with the island expected to require more than 5GW of new electricity sources and power infrastructure to keep up.

Subscribe to our newsletter Taiwan is facing a new challenge in its goal to retain its advantage in AI and advanced semiconductor manufacturing — crucial for maintaining its silicon shield — as it grapples with a tightening power supply. Electricity demand is rising sharply as Taiwanese tech companies build semiconductor manufacturing facilities alongside AI data centers. According to Digitimes, both the AI and semiconductor industries could add about 5 gigawatts of demand — enough to power upwards of 3.75 million homes — by 2030, roughly a gigawatt of additional load per year through the end of the decade.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowThis expansion, fueled by increasing global demand for the most advanced AI chips, is putting even more pressure on the island’s grid. Taipower chairperson Wen-sheng Tseng notes that each wafer fab needs around 200MW to operate, suggesting that the multiple facilities planned for and being built around the island will demand 5.3 to 5.4 gigawatts of electricity. This does not count the other plants and factories needed to support the operation of these factories, so it’s likely that the power demand will even be higher than projected. Nevertheless, four new electricity-generating gas units are set to come online this year, with an additional 5.2GW expected to enter trials soon. The company is also working to beef up Taiwan’s grid and enhance resilience plans, ensuring that the island’s power supply can keep up with the demand, particularly from science parks and industrial zones.Despite that, Taipower is still warning that demand is also increasing outside of these areas. Some fabs and servers are setting up facilities beyond these designated sectors, sometimes even requesting up to 20% of the power consumption of a science park for just a single structure. Because of this, Tseng says that both fabs and tech companies should be careful when selecting sites for their future infrastructure, ensuring that they’ll only be built in places where there is ample power supply to avoid bottlenecks in the future. You may like U.S. electricity grid stretches thin as data centers rush to turn on onsite generators Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption Even though Taiwan’s power utility company is warning about power consumption, it seems to be on track to cover the future growth of the AI and semiconductor industry, provided that the current projections hold. This is a far cry from what the U.S. is experiencing, with the White House resorting to forcing big tech companies to promise that they will pay for the grid and power source upgrades needed to supply the massive amounts of electricity that AI data centers need. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 台湾电力需求预估2030年前增逾5000兆瓦，相当于近400万户家庭用电——半导体制造和AI数据中心部署成主要驱动因素
history.descriptionZh:
台湾在维持其人工智能（AI）与先进半导体制造优势、巩固其“硅盾”战略地位的过程中，正面临新的挑战：日益紧张的电力供应。随着台湾科技公司同时扩建半导体制造设施与AI数据中心，电力需求正急剧攀升。据《电子时报》报道，到2030年，AI和半导体产业预计将新增约5吉瓦（GW）的电力需求——相当于超过375万户家庭的用电量，这意味着直至本年代末，每年将增加约1吉瓦的负荷。

这一扩张主要由全球对最先进AI芯片的强劲需求驱动，正给台湾电网带来更大压力。台电董事长曾文生指出，每座晶圆厂约需200兆瓦（MW）电力才能运营，这意味着全岛规划中和建设中的多座工厂将需要5.3至5.4吉瓦的电力。这还未计入支持这些工厂运营所需的其他配套厂区，因此实际电力需求很可能高于预期。

为应对这一挑战，台湾今年将有四台新的燃气发电机组投入使用，另有5.2吉瓦发电容量预计很快投入试运行。台电同时正努力加强电网建设并提升韧性计划，以确保电力供应能跟上需求增长，尤其满足科学园区和工业区的用电。

尽管如此，台电仍警告称，这些区域之外的电力需求也在增加。部分晶圆厂和服务器设施正选址在规划区域之外，有时甚至单栋建筑就要求占用科学园区高达20%的用电量。因此，曾文生建议晶圆厂和科技公司未来选址需谨慎，应确保只在电力供应充足的地区建设新设施，以避免未来出现瓶颈。

尽管台电对电力消耗发出警示，但若当前预测准确，台湾似乎仍有望满足AI和半导体产业的未来增长需求。这与美国当前境况形成鲜明对比：白宫已不得不强制要求大型科技公司承诺，为满足AI数据中心庞大电力需求所需的电网和电源升级支付费用。

总体来看，台湾在平衡 AI 与半导体产业发展与能源保障方面虽面临压力，但通过积极扩展发电能力与强化电网，正试图为产业持续增长提供支撑，这与部分国家和地区依赖企业承担基础设施升级成本的模式有所不同。

history.title: Trump summons tech giants to White House to pledge power payment commitments — ‘ratepayer protection plan’ will make data center operators negotiate discrete payment structure for electricity use
history.description: Some of the U.S.'s biggest AI tech companies are expected to go to the White House to sign the "ratepayer protection pledge" that promises they will pay for all of their electricity usage and not pass on the burden of increased rates to the average American.

Subscribe to our newsletter The biggest AI tech companies, including Amazon, Meta, Microsoft, and Google, are expected to join President Donald Trump at the White House today to sign the new “ratepayer protection pledge” in a bid to keep electricity prices under control. Trump said during the State of the Union address, “We’re telling the major tech companies that they have the obligation to provide for their own power need.” According to Bloomberg, Meta, Amazon, and OpenAI have confirmed attendance at the White House event on Wednesday, while Google declined to comment.The massive power demands of the AI data centers have upended the electricity market, causing electricity costs for the average American to increase sharply. Some states have reported an increase of up to 36%, with wholesale power prices increasing by 267% in just five years. This is putting undue stress on the ordinary consumer, so much so that both sides of the aisle are demanding action from tech giants.Microsoft was the first to respond to Trump’s call for AI data centers to “pay their own way” when it comes to their electricity consumption, promising to be a “good neighbor” in the communities where it is present. OpenAI soon followed suit, saying that it will fund grid upgrades and apply flexible loads to reduce stress on the grid, while Anthropic said that it will pay 100% of its grid infrastructure costs and even produce new power sources. Now, it seems that the administration wants to formalize these promises. You may like Trump orders Big Tech to build its own power plants to generate power for AI data centers Trump says that AI tech companies need to ‘pay their own way’ when it comes to their electricity consumption Anthropic promises to pay for electricity price increases due to it's AI data centers Energy Secretary Chris Wright reportedly said in a statement that the pledge "will deliver more affordable, reliable, and secure energy for the American people and help stop the rising electricity prices that started during the previous administration," while ensuring the U.S. wins the AI race.However, experts are skeptical whether these promises and pledges will hold water in the long run. “The ratepayer protection plan is a show designed to sweep this issue under the rug and show the White House has solved the problem,” Director Ari Peskoe of the Harvard Law School Electricity Law Initiative told the publication. “The White House has no real authority here aside from the bully pulpit.” It’s not until we see big tech companies sign separate rate structures with electricity providers, grid operators, and state governments that we will know whether these pledges are effective. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 特朗普召见科技巨头承诺付费方案：白宫拟推“纳税人保护计划”，要求数据中心运营商协商独立用电收费方案。
history.descriptionZh:
美国主要科技巨头，包括亚马逊、Meta、微软和谷歌，预计将于今日齐聚白宫，与总统特朗普共同签署一项名为“费率支付者保护承诺”的新协议，旨在控制因人工智能数据中心激增而导致的电价上涨。特朗普在国情咨文中强调，“我们正告知主要科技公司，他们有责任为自身的电力需求负责。”据彭博社报道，Meta、亚马逊和OpenAI已确认出席周三的白宫活动，而谷歌则拒绝置评。

人工智能数据中心的巨大电力需求已严重扰乱电力市场，导致美国普通居民的电费大幅上涨。部分州报告电价涨幅高达36%，批发电价在短短五年内飙升了267%。这给普通消费者带来了过度压力，以至于两党均要求科技巨头采取行动。

微软率先响应特朗普关于AI数据中心应“自负盈亏”的呼吁，承诺在其运营的社区成为“好邻居”。OpenAI紧随其后，表示将资助电网升级并应用柔性负载以减轻电网压力。Anthropic则承诺承担其100%的电网基础设施成本，甚至开发新的电力来源。目前，美国政府似乎希望将这些承诺正式化。

能源部长克里斯·赖特在一份声明中表示，该承诺“将为美国人民提供更可负担、可靠和安全的能源，帮助遏制始于上届政府的电价上涨趋势”，同时确保美国在人工智能竞赛中保持领先。

然而，专家对这些承诺的长期有效性持怀疑态度。哈佛法学院电力法律倡议主任阿里·佩斯科告诉媒体，“费率支付者保护计划是一场作秀，旨在掩盖问题并显示白宫已解决问题。白宫在此事上除舆论影响力外并无实际权力。”只有当大型科技公司与电力供应商、电网运营商和州政府签署独立的费率结构协议时，我们才能判断这些承诺是否真正有效。

history.title: Nvidia invests $4 billion into photonics firms in a bid to bolster data center interconnect supply chains — Lumentum and Coherent investment to fund U.S. R&D and manufacturing facilities, supports capacity rights and future access
history.description: Nvidia has invested a combined $4 billion USD in photonics and networking companies Lumentum and Coherent in a deal that will see both building U.S. facilities, while locking down access rights and capacity for future technologies.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia has pledged to invest a combined $4 billion in two photonic manufacturing firms, Lumentum and Coherent. As Reuters reports, this continues Nvidia's plans to expend capital investment with its expansive cash reserves to help build the AI ecosystem and improve the output of its models. The quiet part is that it also helps keep Nvidia on the cutting edge.Both companies develop optical and semiconductor technologies, enabling future networking hardware and AI chip designs that utilize light to transfer data, rather than electricity. With Nvidia looking to continue providing the digital shovels in this AI gold rush, leveraging forward-looking technologies like photonics may be one way it maintains its edge in the range of hardware spaces it now occupies in the global AI buildout.“AI has reinvented computing and is driving the largest computing infrastructure buildout in history,” said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang. “Together with Lumentum, NVIDIA is advancing the world’s most sophisticated silicon photonics to build the next generation of gigawatt-scale AI factories.” You may like Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneck Marvell’s $5.5B Celestial AI acquisition expands its role in AI data center hardware Nvidia pumps another $2 billion into CoreWeave and announces standalone availability of Vera CPU Jim Anderson, CEO of Coherent, spoke on their deal, stating: “This strategic relationship underscores Coherent’s role as a key enabler of next-generation AI data center infrastructure. We are proud to expand our 20-year relationship with NVIDIA by increasing their access to include multiple product families to help them build the AI data centers of the future.”Cash, access, capacityNvidia has been working with both of these companies for some time on its own networking hardware and to develop a strong supply chain for these key components. These new deals only enhance that cooperation.As with most of the deals within and without the AI industry over the last year, Nvidia's investment in these firms is a mixture of hard currency, future purchase orders, and secured access to key technologies. With Lumentum, Nvidia's $2 billion investment will support its R&D and manufacturing expansion in the U.S. with a new fabrication facility. It also includes a "multibillion purchase commitment" from Nvidia, and "future capacity access rights for advanced laser components."The Coherent deal reads almost identically. There, Nvidia and Coherant talk up the "multiyear strategic agreement" between the two companies, which includes "an Nvidia multibillion-dollar purchase commitment and future access and capacity rights for advanced laser and optical networking products." In addition, Nvidia will also invest $2 billion to support R&D and fab. expansion for its U.S.-based manufacturing facilities.Considering the number of bottlenecks that have appeared since the memory supply shortage began in 2025, from glass cloth to PCB drill bits, Nvidia's investment in expanding photonics development and fabrication in America may be a way to get ahead of future supply constraints, too.Hedging its bets (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)With the similarities between these two investment vehicles for Nvidia, and the near identical nature of the deals and subsequent press releases, it very much feels like Nvidia is hedging its bets. Arguably, that's what Nvidia has been doing since the advent of this major AI revolution.Nvidia is the one major company making money hand over fist in this new era, and instead of hoarding it, or using it exclusively for stock buybacks, or corporate bonuses, it's been reinvesting in the AI industry that put a rocket up its balance sheets and stock price. You may like Nvidia's $20 billion Groq IP deal bolsters AI market domination OpenAI raises $110 billion in largest-ever private tech funding round, Nvidia throws in $30 billion Nvidia's plan to invest $100 billion in OpenAI appears unlikely It invested in Coreweave, OpenAI, Oracle, Intel, Synopsys, and Nokia, all of which are heavily involved in the AI industry or are adjacent to it. It's keenly aware that even as the king of the AI pile, it needs those other economic and industry pillars to keep the whole house of cards standing.If Nvidia were bold enough, it could try to buy out Lumentum and Coherent entirely. Their company valuations are a fraction of Nvidia's profits from even the past quarter. But it didn't. It secured capacity, but not exclusive rights. Nvidia isn't looking to eat the AI industry and supply chain, but bolster its output.It's not a one horse raceAs much as Nvidia is the clear market leader when it comes to AI hardware, it is far from the only player in the game. China has used Nvidia's absence to heavily invest and fuel its domestic inference chip industry. AMD recently signed a $60 billion deal with Meta to power its next-generation AI-powering data centers, and there are small startup companies looking to build out ASIC chips that could prove far more powerful for future AI workloads when fully developed.Marvell's Celestial AI acquisition highlights that other companies see optical technologies as a key future technology for next-generation networking hardware. Meanwhile, the Bill Gates-backed Neurophos is developing AI chips that use photonics to accelerate even beyond what the best GPUs can do.Nvidia made itself the king of the AI hill by having the best hardware at the right time, and as far as training goes, that doesn't seem likely to change any time soon. But if Nvidia doesn't keep developing its hardware, other contenders would emerge. And the competition is ramping up in the inference space, leaving no guarantee that Nvidia will retain its premier position forever.Perhaps getting ahead of the pack when it comes to photonics will be one way it can maintain its lead. But even if it doesn't, $4 billion is a drop in the bucket for a company that made close to 20 times that in the past three months alone.
history.author: Jon Martindale
history.titleZh: 英伟达投资40亿美元强化光子学产业——注资Lumentum与Coherent支持美国研发制造，确保数据中心互联供应链产能与未来供应
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达近日宣布向两家光电子制造企业Lumentum和Coherent各投资20亿美元，总额达40亿美元。这一举措延续了英伟达利用其充裕现金流进行资本投资、构建AI生态系统的战略，同时也旨在通过前瞻性技术布局保持其在AI硬件领域的领先地位。

两家被投公司均专注于光学与半导体技术，致力于开发利用光而非电信号传输数据的未来网络硬件与AI芯片。随着AI引发历史上最大规模的计算基础设施建设，传统电子传输在带宽和能耗方面逐渐面临瓶颈。光子技术通过激光在光波导中传输数据，具有高带宽、低延迟和低功耗的潜力，被视为突破当前AI算力限制的关键路径。

英伟达首席执行官黄仁勋表示，公司将与Lumentum共同推动全球最先进的硅光子技术，以建设下一代千兆瓦规模的“AI工厂”。Coherent首席执行官Jim Anderson则指出，此次合作将强化其作为下一代AI数据中心基础设施关键赋能者的角色。

具体投资结构包含多重维度：除了直接注入资金支持两家公司在美国的研发与制造扩张（包括新建晶圆厂），英伟达还作出了“数十亿美元的采购承诺”，并获得了“先进激光组件及光网络产品的未来产能获取权”。这种“现金+订单+产能锁定”的模式，既保障了英伟达自身未来对关键光子元件的供应链安全，也有助于应对自2025年内存短缺以来从玻璃布到PCB钻头等一系列产业链瓶颈。

从战略角度看，英伟达对两家业务高度重叠的公司进行平行投资，显示出其在技术路线上的风险对冲意图。尽管英伟达完全有能力收购这些规模相对较小的企业，但其选择了非排他性的合作模式，旨在强化而非吞并整个AI供应链。这与英伟达近期对CoreWeave、OpenAI、英特尔等生态伙伴的一系列投资逻辑一脉相承——通过滋养整个AI产业生态来巩固自身的核心地位。

当前AI硬件领域虽由英伟达主导，但竞争正日趋激烈。中国在英伟达芯片受限的背景下大力扶持本土推理芯片产业；AMD与Meta签署了价值600亿美元的AI数据中心芯片协议；Marvell通过收购Celestial AI加码光学互联技术；由比尔·盖茨支持的Neurophos等初创公司也在研发基于光子学的AI芯片，试图实现比现有GPU更强的性能突破。

尽管在AI训练领域英伟达仍占据绝对优势，但在推理市场和其他新兴架构的挑战下，其长期领先地位并非高枕无忧。提前布局光子学这类变革性技术，是英伟达维持竞争优势的重要战略落子。即使未来技术路线发生变化，这笔投资对一家单季利润即超百亿美元的公司而言风险可控，却可能为其赢得关键的未来技术窗口期。

（本文基于Tom's Hardware报道分析生成）

history.author: Stewart Bendle
history.titleZh: 搭配AMD新款9850X3D处理器与X870E主板，仅需201美元即可拥有32GB海盗船复仇者内存——以1,109美元打造炫酷AM5主机。
history.descriptionZh:
知名硬件零售商Newegg日前推出了一项极具吸引力的硬件捆绑促销活动，将构建一台高性能游戏PC的核心组件组合出售，为DIY装机爱好者提供了一个高性价比的解决方案。

此次促销的核心是一个三件套组合，包括：AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D处理器、华硕ROG Strix X870E-E Gaming WiFi主板以及海盗船Vengeance RGB 32GB（2x16GB）DDR5-6400内存套装。该捆绑包的售价为1109.99美元，相比这三件商品单独购买的标价总和1346.98美元，直接为消费者节省了236.99美元，折扣幅度约为18%。

**组件详细解析与技术亮点：**

1.  **AMD Ryzen 7 9850X3D处理器：** 作为该捆绑包的核心，这款处理器基于AM5平台，拥有8个核心。新闻明确指出，凭借略微提升的功耗设定，它已取代Ryzen 7 9800X3D，成为当前市场上游戏性能最快的处理器。其关键创新在于延续了AMD备受赞誉的3D V-Cache技术，通过堆叠超大容量的L3缓存，显著提升了众多游戏的表现，特别是在需要频繁数据交换的场景中能带来更高的帧率。

2.  **华硕ROG Strix X870E-E Gaming WiFi主板：** 这款高端主板是搭配旗舰处理器的理想选择。它获得了Tom's Hardware 4星（满分5星）的评价，被认为是500美元价位段的优秀产品。其主要技术优势包括：**丰富的扩展性**——提供了多达三个PCIe 5.0 M.2 SSD插槽和两个PCIe 4.0 M.2插槽，为未来高速存储升级预留了充足空间；**强大的供电设计**——采用了18+2+2相供电方案，每相可承载110A电流，确保了即使在高负载下也能为处理器提供稳定、纯净的电力；**充裕的连接端口**——配备了大量的USB接口，满足外设扩展需求。

3.  **海盗船Vengeance RGB DDR5-6400内存：** 该内存套装容量为32GB（双通道），运行频率为6400 MT/s。新闻强调，这个频率对于AM5平台的AMD处理器而言是一个“甜点”速度，能在性能、稳定性和兼容性之间取得最佳平衡。海盗船品牌在DIY社区中享有很高的声誉。

**性能与市场背景分析：**
报道通过性能对比指出，Ryzen 7 9850X3D在游戏基准测试中与其前代产品9800X3D成绩接近，但仍能保持微弱的领先优势。这巩固了其作为顶级游戏CPU的地位。

此外，新闻特别指出了当前（指新闻发布时所处的“2026年”市场环境）一个严峻的背景：**内存价格正处在异常高昂的时期**。文中提到，该内存套装单独售价高达437美元，而在此捆绑包中，其折算成本仅为201美元。这反映了全球内存市场供应紧张或成本上涨给PC爱好者带来的财务压力，并建议用户若需购买内存应尽早行动。在此背景下，该捆绑包的性价比尤为突出。

**附加价值与应用场景：**
购买此捆绑包的用户还将获得两份免费赠品：价值55.99美元的华硕TUF Gaming M4 Air轻量化游戏鼠标，以及一份价值70美元的游戏《赤色沙漠》（Crimson Desert）。这进一步提升了整个套餐的附加值。

**总结：**
该促销活动精准定位于追求顶级游戏性能的PC装机用户。它将市场上领先的游戏CPU、扩展性与供电俱佳的高端主板，以及处于“甜点”频率的知名品牌大容量内存相结合，并在内存价格高企的市场环境下，通过捆绑销售提供了显著的价格优惠和额外的游戏外设赠品，构成了一个构建高性能游戏主机的完整、高效且具有成本效益的核心解决方案。

Subscribe to our newsletter Most of the conversation about speed in AI datacenters revolves around the accelerators themselves, discussing tokens per second and the like. However, the battle for AI performance is fought on multiple fronts, and one of them is in memory capacity and power efficiency. Today, Micron unveiled what look to be the industry's first 256 GB SOCAMM2 units, a sizable step up from the last-best 192 GB modules released just six months ago.Go deeper with TH Premium: Memory (Image credit: SK Hynix)AI data centers are swallowing the world's memory and storage supplyThe future of DRAM: From DDR5 to future ICsHigh-bandwidth memory roadmapHere's why HBM is coming for your PC's RAMThe company says it's now shipping samples to customers, who will most definitely be happy at the notion of having 2TB of memory wired to each CPU. For just one major example, the typical Nvidia NVL72 rack can now carry 72 TB of RAM for its 36 CPUs.The 33% improved density over previous-generation SOCAMM2 is excellent news on its own, but that's not the only advantage of this form factor. The new modules ought to offer 66% better power efficiency compared to bog-standard RDIMMs, and they're compatible with increasingly popular (and necessary) liquid cooling for AI servers. You may like Samsung introduces SOCAMM2 LPDDR5X memory module for AI data centers Intel-certified 256 GB DDR5 stick could cut Xeon memory power by 18%, saving millions of dollars Here's why HBM is coming for your PC's RAM According to Micron, the new sticks are the first to employ its 32 Gb (4 GB) LPDDR5X monolithic dies, where "monolithic" means all the memory and relevant circuitry are part of a single die. (Image credit: Micron)Given the target market for these large SOCAMM2s, the firm touts the real-world performance improvements beyond just density and power efficiency. Having this much RAM available to a single processor lets AI models use much larger context windows. Consequently, it helps reduce the all-important TTFT (Time To First Token), meaning bots start answering your questions quicker.In an AI future where context is literally everything, every gigabyte of memory closer to the xPUs in a system matters, and Micron's advancement today will doubtless be found in massive AI server installations worldwide as companies allocate hundreds of billions of dollars of capex in the race toward AI supremacy.The SOCAMM2 form factor is the result of a partnership between Nvidia and memory makers Micron, Samsung, and SK hynix. The SOCAMM standard was originally designed by Nvidia, but the accelerator mogul reportedly had trouble getting the modules to operate without overheating on high-density servers. CEO Jensen Huang wisely teamed up with the folks who make computer memory for a living, resulting in SOCAMM2s with growing density and lower power consumption.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Bruno Ferreira
history.titleZh: 美光率先向客户提供256GB SOCAMM2内存模组样品——数据中心运营商即将实现单CPU 2TB内存
history.descriptionZh:
在人工智能数据中心领域，性能竞争不仅局限于计算加速器本身的内存带宽与算力指标，更延伸至内存容量与能效等关键战场。近日，美光（Micron）正式发布了业界首款容量达256GB的SOCAMM2内存模组，标志着服务器内存技术取得重大突破。该模组距离上一代最高192GB容量的产品发布仅过去六个月，密度实现了33%的显著提升。

此次发布的核心创新在于模组采用了美光新型的32Gb（4GB）单片LPDDR5X内存芯片。所谓“单片”，是指所有存储单元及相关控制电路均集成于单一芯片内，这种设计有助于提升集成度、优化信号完整性与能效。新产品已开始向客户送样，其高密度特性使得单颗CPU可支持的内存容量高达2TB。以典型的NVIDIA NVL72机架为例，其36颗CPU现在可搭载总计72TB的内存。

除了容量飞跃，SOCAMM2形态在能效和散热方面优势突出。相比传统的RDIMM（寄存式双列直插内存模组），新款模组的能效提升高达66%，并且兼容日益成为AI服务器标配的液冷散热方案。这对于功耗巨大的AI数据中心而言，意味着可观的运营成本节约与散热设计简化。

对于AI应用的实际性能，大容量内存带来的直接益处是支持更大的模型上下文窗口。这能有效缩短生成式AI响应请求的“首词元时间”（TTFT），即用户获得AI答复的初始延迟显著降低。在AI推理与训练中，更靠近处理器（xPU）的每一GB内存都至关重要，美光此次技术进步将直接赋能全球各大AI超算集群的建设。

值得注意的是，SOCAMM2标准本身是产业链协同创新的成果。该外形规格源于NVIDIA与美光、三星、SK海力士等头部内存制造商的合作。最初由NVIDIA设计的SOCAMM标准在高密度服务器上面临过热挑战，促使CEO黄仁勋联合专业内存制造商共同优化，最终催生了在密度与能效上持续迭代的SOCAMM2方案。随着全球企业在AI领域投入数千亿美元资本支出，此类高效能内存模组将成为支撑AI算力基础设施演进的核心组件之一。

Subscribe to our newsletter A Google Gemini user has taken to Reddit “in a state of shock and panic.” The issue is with the most recent bill received by their software development business. Redditor RatonVaquero’s typical monthly spend on Gemini AI services is $180. However, in just 48 hours last month, their account “generated $82,314.44 in charges.” A thief has been using the account to generate oodles of Gemini 3 Pro Images and Texts. If Google doesn’t back down regarding these non-trivial fees from the suspected “stolen Gemini API key,” it will bankrupt the company.Tragically, locking the door after the horse has bolted, RatonVaquero has now “Deleted the compromised key, Disabled Gemini APIs, Rotated credentials, Enabled 2FA everywhere, Locked down IAM, [and] Opened a support case.” On the latter point, initial feedback from a Google rep they contacted indicates that the charges will probably stick.From the Redditor’s discussion of their correspondence with Google so far, it looks like the “don’t be evil” company is going to repeatedly cite its ‘Shared Responsibility Model’ for cloud services accounts. I’ve had a quick look at the referenced legal word salad, and I’d guess Google is leaning on the part of its agreement that asks customers to have an authentication system, access policy, and network security in place to protect their API keys, among other things. You may like Google reports that state hackers from China, Russia and Iran are using Gemini in 'all stages' of attacks Alphabet is doubling its capital expenditure to a staggering $180 billion in 2026 AI.com's $85 million Super Bowl ad campaign falls foul as traffic crashes servers Interestingly, though, several Redditors also note that the stolen API key(s) might actually have been there for the taking, and it is Google’s fault for flipping its API key secrecy rules. (Image credit: Google Gemini)Arguing for some ‘mercy,’ RatonVaquero, one of three devs at the affected Mexican development firm, complains that Google doesn’t have “basic guardrails for catastrophic usage anomalies.” The contrast in usage, from a usual $180pcm to $82,000+ in 48 hours, does indeed look like an extreme spike. RatonVaquero also says that there should be features like temporarily freezing services until review and the implementation of per-API spending caps.A look into this overcharging issue indicates that Personal/consumer Gemini customers can’t accidentally spend more than their flat monthly fee. Instead, they have usage caps. Moving up to Dev/Business Google AI Studio users, they can set Quotas (limiting the number of requests per day or per minute). Meanwhile, Google Cloud (Vertex AI) users can set Budget Alerts to notify them when they reach a certain dollar amount.RatonVaquero says they will talk again with a Google rep soon, and have filed a cybercrime report with the FBI. Now they are basically hoping for a softening of big G’s stance. They may be able to share the logs of their unusual “455x spike” in usage, and ask for “goodwill credits” as victims of a cybersecurity incident. It is Kafkaesque, but usually a bit of stubborn persistence can help get your case seen by the right people for a more favorable outcome.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Mark Tyson
history.titleZh: Gemini API密钥被盗两天狂刷8.2万美元，受害者"面临破产"——开发者呼吁设置基本防护栏应对"灾难性异常使用"
history.descriptionZh:
一名谷歌Gemini用户在Reddit上发帖，表示自己“处于震惊和恐慌之中”。问题出在其软件开发公司收到的最新账单上。用户RatonVaquero通常每月在Gemini AI服务上的花费约为180美元。然而，就在上个月的短短48小时内，其账户“产生了82,314.44美元的费用”。这是一名盗贼利用其账户生成了海量的Gemini 3 Pro图像和文本内容。如果谷歌不就这笔来自疑似“被盗Gemini API密钥”的巨额费用让步，将导致该公司破产。

可悲的是，亡羊补牢为时已晚。RatonVaquero现已采取一系列补救措施：“删除了被盗的密钥、禁用了Gemini API、更新了凭证、在所有地方启用了双重验证（2FA）、锁定了身份和访问管理（IAM）、并提交了支持案例”。然而，就最后一点而言，从其联系的谷歌代表处获得的初步反馈表明，这笔费用很可能需要照付。

从该Reddit用户对目前与谷歌沟通情况的描述来看，这家标榜“不作恶”的公司可能会反复引用其云服务账户的“责任共担模型”。快速浏览相关法律条款后可以推断，谷歌很可能依据协议中要求客户建立身份验证系统、访问策略和网络安全措施以保护其API密钥等内容作为依据。

但有趣的是，一些Reddit用户也指出，被盗的API密钥可能本身就被轻易获取，而这要归咎于谷歌改变了其API密钥的保密规则。

作为受影响墨西哥软件开发公司的三名开发者之一，RatonVaquero请求谷歌施以“仁慈”，并抱怨谷歌没有为“灾难性的使用异常设置基本的安全护栏”。使用量从通常的每月180美元激增至48小时内超过82,000美元，这确实是一个极端的峰值。RatonVaquero还表示，谷歌应该提供诸如暂时冻结服务以待审查，以及为每个API设置消费上限等功能。

深入探讨此次超额收费问题可以发现，个人/消费者类Gemini用户不可能意外花费超过其固定的月费，因为他们有使用量上限。对于开发/商业版的Google AI Studio用户，他们可以设置配额（限制每天或每分钟的请求数量）。而Google Cloud（Vertex AI）用户可以设置预算提醒，在达到特定金额时获得通知。

RatonVaquero表示将很快再次与谷歌代表沟通，并已向FBI提交了网络犯罪报告。目前，他们基本上寄希望于谷歌的态度能有所软化。他们或许能够提供显示其使用量出现“455倍激增”的异常日志，并作为网络安全事件的受害者申请“善意抵扣”。这个过程虽然堪称卡夫卡式的荒诞，但通常坚持不懈的努力有助于让合适的人看到你的案例，从而获得更有利的结果。

**事件核心问题分析：**
此次事件突显了几个关键问题：
1.  **API密钥安全管理**：API密钥是访问云端AI服务的凭证，一旦泄露，攻击者即可无限制地滥用，产生巨额费用。用户（尤其是中小型企业）对密钥的保护意识和能力可能存在不足，而云服务提供商的安全策略变更也可能引入风险。
2.  **消费控制与异常监测机制缺失**：在面向开发者和企业的灵活计费模式下，缺乏强制性的、硬性的消费上限设置。虽然提供了配额和警报，但这些属于可选配置，且警报仅为事后通知，无法主动阻止异常消费的发生。服务提供商缺乏对异常使用模式（如短时间内费用激增数百倍）进行自动识别和干预的“熔断”机制。
3.  **责任界定模糊**：云服务商的“责任共担模型”将密钥保护的主要责任置于用户端。但当发生明显异常、可能导致用户破产的恶意消费时，服务商是否应承担部分风险管控失察的责任，或在处理此类事件时展现更大的灵活性，引发了争议。

**潜在影响与行业启示：**
1.  **对开发者的警示**：开发者必须将API密钥视为最高机密，实施严格的访问控制（如最小权限原则）、启用所有可用的安全功能（如2FA），并定期轮换密钥。不能完全依赖服务商的基础保护。
2.  **对云服务商的要求**：此事件可能会促使谷歌及其他云/AI服务提供商（如AWS、Azure、OpenAI等）重新评估其计费安全策略。未来可能会推出更严格的默认消费限制（尤其是对新账户或中小额度账户），增强实时异常检测与自动干预能力，并明确针对“明显欺诈或盗用所致超额费用”的申诉与减免流程。
3.  **行业最佳实践**：预计将推动在开发者社区和中小企业中，更广泛地采用“零信任”安全架构、专用的密钥管理服务以及更精细化的云成本监控与管理工具。

此次事件不仅是单个用户的财务危机，更是对整个基于API的云消费模式安全性的一次拷问，可能加速相关防护措施和行业规范的完善。

history.titleZh: 揭秘：导致人工智能政治抵抗运动的秘密会议内幕
history.descriptionZh:
今年1月初，约90位政治、社区与思想领袖聚集在新奥尔良万豪酒店，参加一场关于人工智能的秘密会议。会议保密程度极高，所有受邀者在踏入会场前均不知晓其他参会者身份。

会议现场呈现出罕见景象：教会领袖与保守派学者邻座，工会代表与科技伦理学家并肩。曾助力伯尼·桑德斯竞选的进步派人士，竟与特朗普支持者呼吸同一片空气。会议发起人——几位AI思想领袖——在紧张氛围中暗自祈祷这场“意识形态熔炉”不会爆发冲突。

这场代号“生命未来”的闭门会议揭示了一个迫切现实：尽管美国社会陷入深度分裂，但跨越政治光谱的群体正形成罕见共识——人工智能将重塑就业、教育、医疗乃至民主制度本身。参会者从基督教广播员到硅谷批判者，从工会组织者到前总统顾问，共同忧虑着AI可能加剧社会不平等、侵蚀人类自主性。

会议核心讨论聚焦三大矛盾：技术乐观主义与人文主义的冲突，算法权力集中化与民主监督的张力，以及AI创新速度与社会适应能力的断层。值得注意的是，极左与极右阵营在“警惕科技寡头垄断”和“保障人类尊严”问题上意外达成共识，这或为未来AI治理的跨党派合作埋下伏笔。

这场秘密集会标志着AI伦理讨论已突破科技圈层，进入更广阔的社会协商阶段。当自由派教师工会领袖与保守派活动家开始使用相同术语讨论算法偏见，当基督教伦理学家与无神论科学家共同起草AI宪章准则，或许暗示着人类社会正在摸索应对技术奇点的新型对话机制。

尽管会议未发布正式宣言，但其象征意义深远：在AI可能重构文明基石的历史节点，传统意识形态对立正让位于更根本的“人类集体生存”议题。这场新奥尔良的密室对话，或许正在悄然绘制未来人机共存时代的权力地图。
